title: sedum telephium subsp. verticillatum
intro: Sedum telephium subsp. verticillatum is a subspecies of the sedum telephium plant, commonly known as white sedum, a perennial succulent native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Sedum telephium subsp. verticillatum is often used in infusions, tinctures, and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
